message:
fix(runtime,security,supply_chain): starvation state-machine tracking, sequence overflow guard, and evidence-bound certification criteria

lane_scheduler.rs:
- Refactored check_starvation() into a proper state machine with a
  StarvationTransition enum (NewlyStarved / Cleared) and a persistent
  starvation_active flag on LaneCounters. Previously, every call to
  check_starvation() while a lane was starved would increment
  starvation_events and emit a duplicate LANE_STARVED audit record,
  making starvation counts unreliable for monitoring. Now the counter
  increments only on the false→true transition, and a new
  LANE_STARVATION_CLEARED audit event fires on the true→false recovery.
- Added comprehensive tests: starvation_events_latch_until_queue_state_recovers
  verifies the full starve→recover cycle and audit trail.

quarantine_controller.rs:
- Replaced saturating_add(1) on the evidence sequence counter with
  checked_add(1) that returns an error on overflow, preventing silent
  sequence number wraparound which would violate the monotonic
  append-only evidence chain invariant.
- Fixed replay_batch() to use each event's own trace_id instead of the
  batch-level trace_id, so audit records correctly attribute evidence
  to their originating trace context.

certification.rs:
- Removed criteria_snapshot_for_level() and stopped rewriting
  satisfied/unsatisfied criteria lists on manual promote/demote.
  The previous behavior was semantically incorrect: promoting an
  extension from Basic to Verified would mark criteria as "satisfied"
  that had never actually been verified by evidence, creating a false
  audit trail. Now criteria remain bound to the original evidence
  evaluation, and only the effective level changes.
- Updated all affected tests to assert criteria preservation semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
